channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Anyone else getting the itch? — 84 replies — 336.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anyone else getting the itch?

Open thread • Replies: 84 • Updated: 2026-03-03 15:59 UTC

OP

The big boys'll go back up, that's guaranteed money. But some others will 10x, 100x, 1000x. I'm feelin' like it's SHIB's turn.

Early replies (first 3)

61900851
>>61899833
Checked

61900872
>>61899833
I'll just keep buying small amounts of it for the rest of my life, and see what happens.

61900948
>>61899833
i believe in shib

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61919529
Shib is fucking dead

61919535
>>61919529
yeah, kinda is
Thankfully i still got in early enough that there was never any chance of not making a profit despite missing out on the big gains through greed but there's nothing but a slow bleed right now and i've seen nothing that means it might pump again

61921116
Bro imagine buying that

61921137
Not today

61921143
No such thing as a free lunch

61921144
>>61899833
none of these shitcoins are gonna do big movements again retard

61921169
>>61921144
No such thing as a free lunch

61921558
Just bought some more boys, let's go

61921644
>>61901257
This already rugged.

61921961
>>61921558
Ummm based?

61921988
>>61921644
Checked and true

61922004
kek turd killer still being shilled here in 2026
lmfao nice AI pics btw

61922014
>>61922004

61922070
>>61922004
You have 2 more weeks wagie

61922078
I hope you don’t buy toad

61922734
No one is buying toad in a shib thread! Shib is going to 14x to ath and toad will go to zero. Shib already has an etf coming and toad has desperate curry flavored shills. LMAO!

61923250
>>61922734
When toad moons YOU'RE going to seethe, when toad moons YOU'RE going to rope!

61923260
>>61899833
I am in anon, people are too stupid to skip this one.
And some Doge in case the elom makes a comeback.

61924787
>>61922734
See
>>61922014

61926036
>>61899833
This thread smells like curry and shit.

Also, shibaggies holding their shibags to zero.

61926122
>>61926036
>This thread smells like curry and shit.
So like most threads on /biz ?

>>61926036
>Also, shibaggies holding their shibags to zero.
Sure buddy. The number 1 meme on eth is going to zero goy!

61927655
>>61926122
>The number 1 meme on eth is going to zero goy!
i'm genuinely considering dumping the rest of what i hold because of this post

61927803
>>61927655
Checked dump it

61927812
>>61899833
you will be lucky to get a 3x

61927816
>>61927812
Shib is ez 10x bear case 33x bull case

61927892
>>61927816
Never happening

61928058
>>61927892
>Shib will never go to a place it’s been before because it just won’t.
You sound tarded bro

61928094
>>61928058
It only went there because of insane hype and FOMO. It will never happen again unless Elon makes it the official currency of X or something

61928421
>>61927655
Please do fud chud. I like cheapies.
>>61927812
>>61928094
He didn't buy cheap before the etf and clarity act passed.
>NGMI!

61929970
> shib niggers in this thread ignoring the most obvious 1000-3000x token right in front of them

item.title: /pmg/ Pirates Metals Goblins — 58 replies — 232.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/pmg/ Pirates Metals Goblins

Open thread • Replies: 58 • Updated: 2026-03-03 15:51 UTC

OP

ARR!

Early replies (first 3)

61926222
yarrr

61926235
>>61926222
arr har har harr! get ye telescope and check this shipmate!

61926254
Arrgh matey, IQCHADJEETs Silva stock bussin nigga frfr on kirk nigga

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61928739
>line go up
>line go down
Honestly, I am at a point where I simply don’t care

61928765
>another gay dip
If you are going to dip, do it to 50$ or don't. I am not buying more "dips" at 80.

61928769
Keep on stacking! I got some 4x 900er silver peace dollars 24,xx g from 1924 and some more few coins in a LCS. (They thin but are huge) I will return, need earn some more money first. ... meanwhile:
As I wrote; Prognose 2026.02.13 for 2026.03.02-03 >>61848331 It happend again just like that.
Not so, funny, facts; Anyone noticed yet that this attack is (almost) in synchronity with this year "Purim" (03.03.2026), also fullmoon+ritualist red moon in some regions.
Also bombing of Jugoslavia was at around Purim 1999.02.28 [Purim for Hebrew Year 5759 began on Monday, 1 March 1999 and ended on Tuesday, 2 March 1999](Fullmoon: Dienstag
2. März 1999, 07:58:30 Uhr),
Iraq 2003.03.20 [Purim for Hebrew Year 5763 began on Monday, 17 March 2003 and ended on Tuesday, 18 March 2003] (Fullmoon: Dienstag
18. März 2003, 11:34:30 Uhr)
and on Ghadaffi/Libia 2011.03.19 (FR)/22 (NATO) [Purim for the year 2011 is celebrated/ observed on sundown of Friday, March 18th ending at sundown on Saturday, March 19th.]
(Fullmoon: Samstag, 19. März 2011, 19:10:06 Uhr) and all on around fullmoon too.
So, just telling, interessting, not? Enjoy the show and be astounished.
There are sure more similar dates/occurencies like the RF Wagner Boss https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yevgeny_Prigozhin and crew died 23.8.2023 in plane bomb/crash on half moon/changing moon day (24.8.2023)
after messed up with RF rulers months earlier.
You might check yourself for more on such. I should have put some bet on this actuall thing, missed opportunity again.
How did I know? Simple by living long enough, experience, and observing patterns and be eager for how mechanics work or not work.
... And what´s up with the China-COMEX demand of silver thing? Nothing happens?

61928790
Stacktards said silver would be $130 today,,,,lmao what is it $70-$75,,,,yes thats what coin shops are offering nigga, on K_rk on G_d

Lesson in that ,,,,,pmg baggies are dumb as fuck,,,,,do the opposite nigga,,,,get rich miga

61928793
OH NO NO NO SILVERSISSIES

61928808
>>61928765
Yeah I'm waiting for a 50 dip to buy again. With how retarded the economy is, I need to start saving fiat again.

61928809
Lord Shortington and Slam Gapore (formally Tam Ping Lo) must have been busy tamping for the blood moon ritual. It went down and faded with the lunar eclipse and bounced back after the moon got out of Earth's shadow. The heavy hitters are usually Tampowitz and Ag Capone. Unless premarket starts at 6AM EST. Then that explains that particularly large dip. Ag Capone, did you get to work at 5AM for a silly occult ritual the same time Shortington was praying to "St." Jimmy Savile next to statues of Gog and Magog? After hours is normally your thing Ag Capone.

61928826
>>61928809
Lord Shortington was the true star today, so far.

61928839
>>61928826
Yall laughed when Petey B shorted at $117 nigga,,,,,called him a joo,,,,who's laughing now nigga?

61928841
>>61928790
It normally creeps up a few dollars a day naturally. Someone would have to buy a year's worth of production at once to get up that fast. Only paper silver pumps and dumps like a shit coin.

61928848
>>61928841
>It normally creeps up a few dollars a day naturally
There are entire decades where it just creeps down, zoom out. If you aren't in the game to have a shiny collection and larp for decades, you're about to be really buttmad for a long time.

61928859
fuck we're not getting out of the boo box today are we

61928867
>>61928826
I would be surprised if the market was open on Purim in any western city. Or was it the shabbos goys in lambskin aprons doing it? Because "Dude! Blood Moon!".

61928870
>>61928841
Paper is cheaper than physical because you have to pay 20% premiums,,,,people who buy physical are broke retards on k_rk

61928874
Afternoon m8ys
Excellent to see the Captain back at the helm.

61928884
>>61928841
>Only paper silver pumps and dumps like a shit coin.

Talking about shitcoins good Ol' Black Rock one of the biggest real estate assets collectors is starting to liquidate its Bitcoin assets. Doesn't seem like it's a good year to be a crypto bro.

61928922
>>61928867
Blood moon, im gonna COOM!

61928930
>>61928870
The premiums are normally lower. When things are unstable they get very high.

>>61928848
I am talking about $10+ pumps and dumps happening in a single day. That is not natural. If this dropped $15 dollars in a month it would be believable. But you can't even do that because of real supply and demand for silver. And everyone can see when the big dumps happen and figure out who is doing it based on time zone. Cartels killing Mexican miners and silver bombs being dropped on Iran is now added onto your cheap silver tamping problems.

61928997
>>61928930
You're forgetting about Jane Street recently becoming one of the biggest Silver Futures holder right now. You know, the same one being investigated by India and China.

https://coinpedia.org/news/why-is-silver-falling-jane-streets-1-3b-slv-bet-sparks-manipulation-debate/
https://www.reuters.com/world/india/jane-street-deposits-567-million-escrow-accounts-per-india-regulatory-directives-2025-07-14/
https://archive.is/wip/dCGDy

Fuckers are even beating Black Rock. Trying to apply technical analysis to this market while fun is ultimately an exercise in futility if you don't know all of its key players suppressing the price. The only good position in Silver is owning it physically.

61929008
>>61928997
>The only good position in Silver is owning it physically.


The choice of broke retards,,,,no thanks shill

61929016
>>61928930
>Cartels killing Mexican miners and silver

The price dropped $20 yall said this would make it go up $30,,,,lmao yall learned nothing and still claim to nostradamus

61929416
>>61929008
Most of us bought years ago. Myself included. (And yes I did sell a few ounces when it was over $80 a while ago.) The rest are people willing to buy at a loss or are retards. I am here to watch for fun. You are not stressing me out. I wait for Comex to be found empty with glee.

61929468
>>61929416
>And yes I did sell a few ounces when it was over $80 a while ago.
absolutely no shame in that. It's quite literally what silver is for. You buy it when the price is low, build up your stack, and once the GSR closes down like it did recently you sell off no more than half of your stack and convert the FIAT you receive into free gold.
Holding silver forever hoping it will make you rich one day is the same as buying bitcoin nowadays and hoping for the same.
Rinse and repeat.

61929486
About to try and get a refund from TD, been 36 business days and no confirmation of my 4 bars being shipped. They’re gonna try and say some final sale bullshit, but they’ve not held up their part of the “contract”. I stupidly grabbed 5 one ounce silver maples 2 weeks ago, might try to lump that in with the refund (x to doubt for this part being refund) cause I could just run and slurp those 5 locally for same price I paid on TD. Inb4 I get the refund and silver moons before I get the cash back in my account to go buy at my LCS

61929507
I considered selling a little silver for gold recently but quite like that Clive Thompson characters approach if at least waiting for 30to one instead of 50plus. It's nice to have a little pike of silver anyway even if I prefer gold. So atm I'll jyst dca more

61929573
>>61929468
Didn't sell my gold. Not buying gold right now. Maybe in a few years I will buy some bling. Waiting for stock market cheapies and pampering myself this month. Thanks occult retards. You are so predictable. It is fun to find out how many of you blackmailed ziontards own shares stocks I still hold.

61929582
>>61929573
please rewrite that in English and without the schizobabble

61929747
>>61929582
People who got rich have multi generational wealth spend money to cause things to fulfil prophesies because they are bored. It works until people catch on and can figure out when they buy and sell stuff. Purim blood moon at 3:33 AM PST? Massive sell off. Sell some of your stocks and before that day especially institutional investor heavy stocks. Then wait for cheapies in things you were eyeing. Just bought a share of an oil stock that dumped. Makes no sense that it would dump when it was showing the start of a long slow upward trajectory before a classic bull run. And that was before the Iran thing. Oil stock are hardly dumping because they shouldn't be. Aware people are buying today while they dump good stocks for no apparent reason because of dumb rituals. I will wait much longer for other things. I know this could go on for years.

61929770
>>61926208
Anyone else remember AYY lmao coin anon?
Had to sell all his coins because of a family emergency like 2 years ago

I still think about this alien coins he had some fucking cool shit
Poor fucker missed the pump

61929916
>>61929486
Anyone ever tried or had luck with refunding from TD precious metals? Shithawking the chart before I send off my email, knowing today it’ll pull some Talmud magic and moon when I ask for a refund/get refunded

Here we go!

Early replies (first 3)

61891698

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61903514
>>61897932
Stop giving jews money

61904108
I bought the pico bottom but only threw in $1000

61904711
>>61904697
HOLY FUUUUCK
OOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

61904727
>>61891742
>Ripple Labs will buy PayPal. Screen Cap this.
I don't think so, only because they claimed they didn't have any big acquisitions planned for the rest of the year

61908061
>>61903001
739M SHARE

61909665
no merger.
we are fucked

61909693
>>61909665
the gap will be filled

61909824
>>61904697

61910089
>>61906351
Thanks for playing, baggies.

61910124
>>61909824

61915327
Any news?

61916113
>>61915327
https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/filing-intech-investment-management-llc-purchases-30734-shares-of-paypal-holdings-inc-pypl-2026-02-28/

61918275
Is this still going to 60$? I went all in at 46 because of this thread.

61919240
>>61918275
I Hope return at price of institutional have buy

61919338
>>61892826
>$300
impressive

61919350
>>61918275
>d
the CEO will get his big cut only at $83 dollar share price MINIMUM.
look at warner bros that shit went from below $10 to now beeing baught at $30
If Paypal gets aquired it will be atleast $100

61919773
>>
https://hedgefollow.com/stocks/PYPL
Hope

61921315
>>61919350
I doubt it will go that high.

61923340
>>61923318

61923342
>>61923340

61925981
>>61904727
At the rate they were going, it wasn't going to be a big acquisition, merely a ripple on the pond

61926795
AMZN

61927102
Can someone explain why Paula even exists?? I remember you needed it for eBay back when everything was new. Somehow it morphed into the default asking to payment

61928616
>>61927102
It exists because easy cc payment processor services didn't exist for a long time. You used to give your cc info directly to the web vendors at payment, which eventually got people scammed on shady websites. Paypal was the first of its kind there. The internet has somehow COMPLETELY forgotten that money used to be a complete pain in the ass to move around. Btc, being somewhat clunky now, was actually a huge move in being able to transfer money without oversight or other friction overhead. Now that its been incorporated into the collectivist panopticon, both btc and PayPal are harder to use than just years ago

item.title: I do love how boomers, because of their calcified brains, just keep on dumping money into housing because it's a "safe i… — 28 replies — 112.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I do love how boomers, because of their calcified brains, just keep on dumping money into housing because it's a "safe i…

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-03-03 16:01 UTC

OP

I do love how boomers, because of their calcified brains, just keep on dumping money into housing because it's a "safe investment".

When they started buying housing in the 1970s, the land to structure value ratio was 1 : 4, now it's reversed and is like 3 :1 in the opposite direction. A $800,000 property has like $250,000 in the structure and the other $550,000 is the value of the land itself. But when you plug in the 1970s ratio it's like $70,000 for the land. And you can't say it's because land is scarce, because India is much more crowded and their land is worth fuck all.

Housing prices here have crashed 30% in real terms since Covid and oblivious boomers STILL think "housing only goes up" and are dumping their remaining money into real estate. The only reason I think the prices have crashed to the degree they have is because a lot of boomers literally died off or ended up in the rocking chair.

They apparently got traumatized by a stock market crash in the 1980s and since then have been risk adverse to any investment that isn't real estate. Which is fucking stupid. Real estate is literally a DEPRECIATING ASSET. How fucking retarded do you have to be to assume it'll just keep on doubling in value forever.

A lot of the housing they're living in is completely fucked too. Old ladies that live from 65-85 functionally stop doing repairs in the last ten years of their life, so by the end you're just left with a moldy rotting mess.

Early replies (first 3)

61928209
>buy modest home for $125k in 2019
>have over $200k in PMs in 2026
If my home goes to $25k, that means others in the neighborhood are doing the same. I'll just buy more houses and rent them out.

61928240
I live in a house that requires two college graduate salaries. Even the most industrious beaner with a lawn care company can't afford to be my neighbor. I haven't heard Spanish in 6+ months unless it's from someone I have paid to be there to do something menial.
I don't even remember what black people look like

61928243
The internet is filled with financially illiterate zoomers who don't understand what inflation is.

>Inflation is down, then why are things still expensive
>Housing prices have gone up through out all of human history, but in 2026 it's going stop going up because some brown incel on /pol/ said so

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61928246
Even if the value of a boomer's house went up 2% a year, that doesn't offset the time and cost of mowing the lawn every week.

61928250
>>61928192
historically it does go up, doesn't mean there aren't peaks and crashes

61928265
in my retarded fucking cunt, sPAIN, we are no longer building new buildings while importing millions of immigrants each year.
i dont think housing prices are going down any time soon, theres infinite demand and zero supply.

61928281
>>61928265
Those thirdies will, after a certain point, just not comply to western building standards and regulations, and start living in literal trailers and shacks on land. They're already compressing 6 of them into a bedroom like sardines, that'll just get worse.

Already in Canada you're starting to see actual encampments being built on Public land, with the government basically giving up on clearing them. That's how the Brazilian Favelas got their start.

61928328
>>61928243
>All of human history
Wrong. Housing was fucking expensive in England in the 1500s, but in the 1600s it was as cheap as permission to join a charter to the New World. Housing was fucking expensive in the 1930s but post war saw housing go down thanks to mass housing projects. Prices fluctuate. Homes aren't any different.

61928639
>>61928281
>indian favelas located on public streets
Trudeau will eventually be remembered as the grim reaper of canada. But he got a Freemason slave family out of it, so maybe it was worth it to him

61928662
>>61928192
You can either play the game or keep whining about those that do and lose.

61928666
>>61928246
Mowing is fun though

61928675
This is adjusted for inflation, London is so fucked lmao, turns out no one wants to live with foreigners

61928694
>>61928675
Oh and you could see this as a good correction since gdp per capita has been stagnant for like 20 years so it is good that 20 years of growth in house prices has been wiped out (though gotta suck for the sellers that have held for a long time and have to pay capital gains tax when they havent earned anything in real terms lmao)

61928723
/biz only seethes at homes so much because of sour grapes

any brown retard can download an app and buy $50 of a memecoin. but to buy a house you either need tons of cash or a good job and good credit. you can't buy $50 worth of house

the neets, jeets, and larpers of /biz/ are quite literally priced out

61928731
>>61928675
That's just rich people fleeing UK because of new taxes or something

61928736
>>61928723
Young people also hate the idea of being tied to one place. But by the time they move from one place to another about 10 times, they'll want to get off the rentie carousel and settle down with a nice house

61928766
>>61928736
Zoomies can do math Boomer, owning a house is a crappy investment right now. My rent is cheaper than my insurance + property taxes + amortized interest and that's with a 150k downpayment (that most people don't have). I have that 150k in the market and it made 13% last year between equities and CDs/Bonds, houses in my area went up 0.7% yoy, they literally lost value relative to inflation.

61928786
>>61928766
At the same time, you’re renting. What do you get in the end? A pat on the back? With buying you actually have something.

61928794
>>61928786
His argument is that he "gets" the return on his investments that would otherwise be tied up in the house. It makes sense, but probably only applies in certain areas. At least where I live, mortgage payments are lower than rent.

61928820
>>61928240
/thread

61928835
>>61928766
>made 13% last year
And how much did rent go up? Substract that from your profits, retard. You will keep getting gradually priced out of renting every year. Renting never gets cheaper. Even when housing gets cheaper, renting somehow doesn't

61928903
>>61928731
Obersturmführer Keir Starmer took office in july 2024, it was crashing well before that

61928911
>>61928835
Supply and demand actually works on rents too

61929054
>>61928192
Home prices aren't coming down, rates aren't either. You waited for a repeat of something isn't going to happen again

61929310
>>61928835
>And how much did rent go up?
So you acknowledge that i am making a profit relative to buying? I'm on a month to month lease, rents actually went down in my local area but I'm the retard still paying the same 2023 prices instead of leaving my rented duplex. Say how are your property taxes trending?

>>61928786
>At the same time, you’re renting. What do you get in the end? A pat on the back? With buying you actually have something.
Have what a larger property taxes bill? Enjoy getting scammed by your local government for an "asset". I wonder how much loss the housing market will suffer with restricted immigration and millions of dying boomers whose heirs will dump their three houses onto the market. With renting, I have growth in my equities and CDs, if I realized just last years gains I'd have almost 20k for doing nothing, that's like double your salary.

61929671
>be me
>the prophet has spoken
>life is meaningless without Keys and Za
>mortgage? eternal debt? irrelevant
>what matters is holding the sacred ring of Keys in one hand and the greasy Disc of Za in the other
>ancestors built cathedrals, empires, dynasties
>I will build equity… maybe… after 27 years of payments
>but today I ascend, for today I post Keys and Za on plebbit
>thousands of upvotes, dopamine sacrament, communion in tomato sauce
>I am complete, I am saved, I am His disciple

hail Keys, all hail Za

61929841
>>61928192
Sounds like cope. I got a house 2 years ago with zero down payment, and a mortgage with $150 lower monthly payments than I used to rent a shitty studio for. 2.15% APR. Feels comfy, I already built up almost 100k in equity by paying less than rent.

61929982
OP has no idea what he is talking about.
First off, the boomers who were invested in stocks made bank in the 1980s, even though there was that short lived crash in 87. it was the dot.com bubble crash in 2001 that spooked them.

Secondly, the tax benefits of real estate are significant and the leverage is key.
>buy $500,000 house
>put $50k cash in as DP
>house goes up in value by 5% every year.
>pay $30k every year in PITI
>year 1 ~RoC = 31%
>at the end of ten years, home value is $825k
>you have put $350k into it
>all of your interest was tax deductable
> so over 10 years, you saved ~$40k in taxes
>10 year return on cash is 104%
>you also got to live in it, so didn't pay rent
>10 years of rent at avg $3000/month = $360,000
>Adjusted 10 yr RoC = 207%
And if you sell it, the proceeds are tax free, increasing the value of the investment at sale by 30% or more.

Now do it as an investment:
>$350k rental house = 20% DP = $70k cash
>assume net even cash flow year 1; rents increase 5%/yr, so rent 2400/mo Year 1
>costs stay relatively stable year over year (mortgage stays the same, but Insurance; taxes; repairs increase 5%/yr
>yr 2: rent = $2520; costs = $2441
>yr 2 cashflow = +$950
>end of yr 2 value = $385,875
>RoC = 52%
>yr 10 rent = $3900/month
>yr 10 expenses = $2900/mo
>yr 10 value = $570k
>cumulative 10 year cashflow (income) = $60,000 (tax deferred bc of "depreciation")
>Loan principal paid by renters = $70,000
>10 year total return = $350k
>10 yr RoC = 500%
>if you sell, about 50% of proceeds will be taxed at a reduced LT Capital Gains rate, netting you an additional $17k

item.title: Passive Money Farming / Side Hustles While on the Clock — 41 replies — 96.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Passive Money Farming / Side Hustles While on the Clock

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-03-03 15:37 UTC

OP

>Have an easy office job
>Mostly just scroll on my phone
>Literally just do barely anything and make salary
>Realize I could make money in my phone during this time
>Start making AI art, get up to $100 a month from subs
Anyone aware of something that could make more than this and is primarily done on your phone. I do not mind doing it on my PC at home too, as I already do this with AI stuff For now, AI is easy enough and extra money is extra money but more cash flows will always be better
Note, I want actual income, not “Just sell options on your phone while at your job bro”

Early replies (first 3)

61924922
So basically asian tits make me straight haha

61924932
I'm considering getting a 3d printer farm plus I know how to work with the files and make custom things but idk seems effort

61924936
>>61924922
I’m happy for you anon

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61925611
>>61925594
High seven figure hell is real. I want to quit working but Canada is stupid expensive to live in. Probably gonna fuck off to the Caribbeans once I hit $1 million USD

61925619
>>61924922
You're 35. It's time to grow up and have a wife AND kids.

61925623
>>61925611
Oops meant high six figure.

61925624
>>61925611
Well the numbers are based on how much you can extract monthly while still living like a human and not like a nigger so yeah

61925650
I was considering candle making but it's saturated by women

61925663
Hey man, I know it's difficult, but listen, the honest truth is that most phone based side hustles that feel easy are capped low and crowded, so your hundred a month from AI art is actually normal, not underperformance, and the uncomfortable reality is that meaningful extra income usually comes from either building an audience, selling something people repeatedly buy, or doing work others are too lazy or too skilled to do, which means if you want to beat that number from your phone you should focus on things like running niche subscription pages with consistent content drops, flipping underpriced items locally through marketplace apps, managing short form content pages that you later monetize with affiliates, or doing simple but in demand freelance tasks through mobile friendly platforms, because the people who quietly make real side money are not chasing magic passive tricks, they are stacking small repeatable systems and letting them compound while everyone else keeps searching for effortless cash.

61925701
>>61925560
I’m a doctor and found a woman similiar to that but white. I already left her tho because i want to fuck differebt kinds of hotties. So whatevet doctor makes + social status.

61925705
>>61924882
i fucking NEED a gf this hot
how do i do it ? money? looksmaxing?
please someone tell me how

61925774
>>61925705
All you’re going to notice is that boobs are just blobs of fat that smell like fat and look way better than they feel. Also the woman will naturally be rather unimpressed by anything because of how hot she sees herself.

61925778
>>61925705
She's not hot, she just has big tits

61925779
>>61925564
Don't ever say dumb shit like that ever again. Lack of money destroys even the strongest love.

61925806
>>61924882
Making money off of a phone alone just means you are a content creator. Which is a viable side hustle if you have charisma and/or skills and knowledge. Everyone wants a job where they make money with their phone so try something else.

And it's nice having a big titty wife.

61925811

61925831
>>61925774
i dont care all women are whores anyway so i might as well have a hot big titted gf
>>61925778
you are either a homosexual or a jealous roastie

61925844
>>61925705
>>61925774
You don't NEED one anon. Remember you will find no peace with a "hot" woman. Every guy even your friends will be constantly trying to fuck her or messaging her. This will keep you in a state of perpetual paranoia and misery. Hot women are mostly unhinged and mentally unstable. They expect you to do everything in the relationship while offering very little in return. Probably boring unenthusiastic sex too. She will most likely abuse you in the relationship because she sees you as wasting her time when she could be out fucking other chads and gaining maximum attention for her herself. Hot women are out for themselves they know that they have millions of thirsty guys who will fuck them, and they will make you aware of that every day.

61925851
>>61925663
great post

61925903
>>61925806

61925909

61925926

61925995

61925999

61926357

61926427

I’m fairly content and have a friend who is making more so if I can scale to 150 or 200 monthly that’s really not bad all things considered

61926915
I didn't read shit bitch. I just saw tits.

61926931

61928037

item.title: Does IQ make you more successful financially? — 35 replies — 93.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Does IQ make you more successful financially?

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-03-03 15:40 UTC

OP

Does IQ make you more successful financially?

Early replies (first 3)

61927833
>>61927814
No, but emotional intelligence does.

61927872
>>61927833
>emphaty
retard detected

61927878
>>61927814
>>61927833
>>61927872


now post her without make up and filters and 0.1sec clips

i'll wait

fucking yellow fever fags

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61927916
>>61927814
I'm pretty confident I wouldn't be a millionaire now if I didn't have a strong background in math. That strong background generally requires a higher than average IQ.

61927931
FOMO is an anxiety. If you can't reflect and regulate your own emotions you will lose money.
https://youtu.be/bEXz7Abf-wM?si=5Fm5SX09qPE0ycgq

61928032
The four cardinal virtues are also a helpful concept. Achieving them is a sign of maturity.
-fortitude
-justice
-temperance
-prudence

61928033
>>61927814
No, self-control and practical mathematics applied to real-world problems and risk management are what make you successful relative to your peers.

You can be high-IQ and have a very strong grip on abstract mathematics, while having zero emotional control or risk management.

Personality is more of a predictor of your success than IQ. But, a higher IQ can equal a higher income, but not more wealth, because you may blow your income on consumable slop, or lose it for whatever reason.

I'm 32 years old and work a basic factory wagie job in the rural Midwest. I'm also in the top 7% of networth for people my age, because I'm extremely good with finance and have the right personality for it.

OCEAN Test Results:

O = High
C = High
E = Middle
A = Low
N = High

61928043
>>61928033
this

61928081
>>61927814
God I need a plastic chinkgook like this to step on me

61928334
>>61927833
>Motivation
What does that have to do with emotional intelligence? Even retards who can never read the room can have great motivation.

61928351
>>61928081
watch out. she will spank you with her spring onions, anon.

61928387
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Emotional_intelligence

61928394
What if somebody wants to learn the latin language and shit. Maybe they font want to optimize the tiktok.

61928410
>>61927814
Yes, everything else is cope and lies of low IQ people who really want to be high IQ and tells that kind of lies to them selfs.

61928414
Asian pussy makes you successful. That's why WMAF is the future.

61928426
>>61927833
>Motivation
All is vanity as the preacher said. I can do things though but my heart will not be in any of them
>Social skills
Yes, i have them but i hate people and choose to avoid them like the plague
>Empathy
Real intelligence entails high levels of empathy. I get you're sad but i won't cry with you because your cat has died. I don't expect you to cry if my father dies. I won't. It's the circle of life, Simba.
>Self awareness
I can read a room perfectly enough to know when my antisemitic jokes haven't landed as i expected
>Self regulation
Buzzword i don't know what it means. I can regulate and adjust myself according to the situation.

61928448
>>61927814
I have ~130IQ and i'm a poorfag NEET college dropout at 24. I have very little self-control or motivation so it's hard to get and hold a job, and I am very socially awkward so it's hard to make connections (the only way to get any skilled job nowadays), so i ended up an outcast. Many people in that range seem to have similar issues.

However, if you have 110-120IQ you probably will be "normal" enough to not be a weird outcast and smart enough to get ahead of most normalfags, and if you're >145IQ you're a genius and will probably end up being world-class at something.

I'm not trying to sadpost or get sympathy, it's just what I've observed.

61928455
>>61928448
Yea being king of the midwits is the best, you just crash out if you have le intelligence and realize we live in hell

61928672
>>61927833
Every billionaire is a psycho that eats the babies he just raped and the goyim be like
>duuuude just be nice and money will come to you :)

61928885
>>61928448
I'm sorry man. Whenever I meet academic people they seem to be not that bright. Often they can only do that one thing they are specialized and that's it. They just had that motivation to study hard but they are not that smart. Still they make make good money. They just fit into the system and profit from it.

61928893
>>61927814
IQ is simply the ceiling for skill and knowledge. It alone doesn't guarantee anything.

61929003
>>61928885
That's because academic people *aren't* that bright. The median IQ of college professors is only 128, only slightly higher than medical doctors (124).
>>61928448
I have a 161 IQ and I'm also a college dropout.
>if you're >145IQ you're a genius and will probably end up being world-class at something
I wouldn't say I'm world-class at anything except shitposting on 4chan, but I think the extra IQ means it's easy to do well without really trying. The place I was working had spent years and millions of dollars trying to solve some knotty problems and had made no progress despite having a PhD and several mensa members on the team. I happened to overhear some discussion about it in the lunchroom and before I finished my ham sandwich I had figured out the solution, which led to me getting promoted to the technical lead on that team and making pretty good money, a decade later I'm in the top 1% in net worth in the US, all for a whopping 5 minutes of actual thinking.

61929120
IQ doesn't exist.

The only thing it tests, is if you are cognitively retarded or not. But then again, basically any type of test can determine that.

The only reason people still talk about IQ is because people's fragile ego's and political dogmas hinge upon it. Not because it has any scientific merit.

61929203
>>61929120
>reeee this thing I don't have doesn't ackshually exist
It's funny because you think you're being clever about it, but it's all just so transparent and sad.

61929223
>>61929203
It's funny how you confirmed exactly what he said with your little emotional outburst.
I have no idea what my IQ is myself, never tested it. All the smart guys I know never tested their IQ either, if you care about it, that's already a sign you're an insecure midwit

61929256
>>61927814
how the fuck would i know

61929257
>>61929120
t. 94 IQ faggot who thought he was the next Einstein before taking the test

61929269
>>61929223
Actual smart guys were taken by their parents to a psychologist for IQ testing in grade school though, it's a necessary step for skipping grades and early graduation/college.

61929405
>>61929257
>>61929203
This is always the boring predictable response from losers and failures with no achievements in life, and no real talents. Just to bait me into revealing that I happened to score very highly on the IQ test.

Lack of introspection is a sign of low intelligence though. So naturally when you got *your* bullshit score, you didn't question it for a second, and proudly displayed it on your reddit profiles.

61929628
>>61929405
>So naturally when you got *your* bullshit score, you didn't question it for a second
this is evidence you never did an IQ test in your life

61929644
>>61929269
Why are you trying to proof he had no high IQ? You are proofing him again by doing it. "people's fragile ego's and political dogmas hinge upon it." :)

61929692
>>61927814
does pooping make women smell better?

61929814
Get a load of this young lady.
(temperance)

>>61928593

61928691

61929067
>>61928593
relax. its a discount. might even get better in a bit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61929104
>>61928593
this makes no sense, people buy metals in times of war ffs... are normies buying natural gas or something?!

61929133
>>61929104
everyone is still buying metal. consumer habbits dont effect the price of metal. the price manipulation is being played on a different level.

im playing chess, my opponent pulls a gun on me, this does not effect his next turn in the game but it effects reality on a different level.

61929165
>>61929133
>THE PRICE ISN'T REAL!
>THE REAL PRICE IS IN MY HEART!
silver baggies lol

61929181
>>61929165
>burying your head in the sand

61929189
>>61929181
>HE POSTED IT AGAIN
HAHAHAHAHAHAHA
reminder that when you sell, it will only be for spot price

61929192
>>61929165
just bought more bobo... seeth more maybe?

61929199
>>61928632
Too bad that's ALSO dropping hard like shiny rocks

61929220
>>61928593
It actually makes a lot of sense you absolute retards. In hard uncertain times, where oil price soars, funds need to grab a lot liquid cash quickly to better secure leverages. The best thing they have to sell is boomer rocks

61929686
>>61929104

It only makes sense if the government is stable. When the government is broke and it goes to war, there's a good chance that they will take your shit.

61929697
>>61929104
>this makes no sense, people buy metals in times of war ffs..
That's what they tell the goyim lel

61929732
>>61928593
>TASE +6%
Thanks for playing, goyim!

61929737
>>61929165
The spot price is clearly not real.
We’re still up 200% in the last 5 years.
We were 100% right.

61929744
I bought the gold dip, thanks jews

61929760
>>61929737
Meanwhile………

61929766
>>61928593
Liquidity, the shorts are moving to oil now in order to fuck with that market. The shorts being distracted will allow gold/silver to rebound properly. Then again idgaf I'm playing the long game, and trying to predict pm market with technicals when there's decades upon decades of history of manipulation is dumb.

61929809
>>61929165
The real price is actually in Shanghai, still over 90$.

61929834
>>61929744

The dips and pumps are fake, you're buying the change in the perceived value of the dollar. Rock fags are low IQ.

Enjoy the treadmill workout, nigger.

61929849
>>61928593
I voted for this

61929879
>>61929760
>but what about muh bitcoin
Bitcoin's spot price isn't real. It's obviously 10 gorillion dollars and da joos are slamming the price!!!

item.title: Hyperliquid >decentralized perp and spot exchange >trading silver, gold and crude oil on weekends during a war >genius l… — 39 replies — 48.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Hyperliquid >decentralized perp and spot exchange >trading silver, gold and crude oil on weekends during a war >genius l…

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-03-03 15:15 UTC

OP

Hyperliquid

>decentralized perp and spot exchange
>trading silver, gold and crude oil on weekends during a war
>genius level founders. Harvard, MIT and caltech.
>no vc funding from day 1
>31% geneis airdop tokens to users minting millionaires for early usrs.
>97% all platfrom fees spent and buy HYPE tokens from open market and sent to burned wallet.


Holding HYPE is comfy AF

Early replies (first 3)

61918612
>>61918597
Buy an ad.
CLEAN IT UP JANNIES

61918623
>>61918597
>Shilled by literally all of Twitter fags including scammers like Arthur
>Token not needed
Yeah, no.

61918628
>>61918623
why don't people say "stock not needed". its a crypto company and the coin is basically the stock.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61919310
>>61918612
HYPEBTC has a nice bullflag setup

61919323
>>61918623
>token not needed

All 97% of all fees generated by the Hyperliquid exchange is used to buy HYPE tokens and gets sent to burn address.
$1.3 Billion is fees so far. All on chain.

0xfefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefe

61919354
>>61919323
Is the hype token used as gas for the chain?

61919376
Fuck hyperliquid, they forced cz the good guy to giganuke the market (like he did in 2022 to destroy ftx/sbf), but these fuckers are still alive.
They need to disappear so cz can allow the market to recover.

61919383
>>61919376
I don't hold any hype but I use the exchange on a daily basis and it works better than even centralized exchanges. It's the best product out there.

61919391
>>61919354
Yes. Hype is used as gas on the HyperEVM chain.

There are two chains in Hyperliquid ecosystem.

HyperCore - trading engine. No gas needed. Pay with trading fees which is used to buy and burn HYPE.

HyperEVM - smart-contract layer. HYPE used as bas.

61920112
I was thinking about grabbing a bag in late Jan and then it pumped again. Serves me right for wanting to buy this under 20 burgerbux

61920124
>>61920112
Same, bro. I just hold btc and trade on hyperliquid. I wanted to get myself a good back of hype, it's the best project out there and the team are not niggers. They want hype to go up. This weekends pump is because everybody is using it to trade crypto and commodities.

One of the few chains that is actually used. No marketing, no bullshit kantian philosophy or any kind of german idealism talk like Sergey or Charles hoskinson

61920169
How do I even buy Hype in the US? I need to use a VPN?

61920469
>>61919376

61920537
can i trade spot btc on there?

61920572
>>61920537
yes. Crypto, stocks, commodities... both spot and perps

61920574
Forex too

61921651
>>61920169
it's on Coinbase now starting Feb 2026. Not sure if it's available in USA.

VPN is very useful. expressvpn

61921686
Funny , this thread popped up because hype's about the moon

61921966
>>61918612
tell that to bloomberg you stupid nigger

61921981
>>61918623
>hurr im not going to buy this multi-billion marketcap token because [insert random person i dont like] is in it!!!
You are thinking like a pleb here and will simply miss out on profits this way.

61922017
>>61918597
I only have 250 hype and refuse to buy up here. It has to retrace back to the low 20's at some point.

Also shilling hype here is a waste. This entire board is so underwater on their bags they're in the mariana trenches.
>Link generals
>Avi generals
>BBBY/GME

I said this to myself earlier this year but Jeff and Vitalik are truly the last 2 people in this entire fucking space that still hasn't soul their souls yet

61922034
>>61921981
Look up GMX.

61924490
Sigh
These poor souls

61924506
edgex is better

61924534
>>61921981
why would he want to buy something that is already multibillions?

61924803
>>61918597
>sent to burned wallet.

why are you lying? the tokens arent burned, they are kept in their treasury

they probably will never have a need to touch those tokens, but they arent burned

61924887
>>61918707
So, the gas is, on a direct level paid in USDC. This is why you one-time sign, and can trade without having to sign each time, unlike GMX. Your trading fees are gas.
These trading fees are used to buy & burn hype. That's why the exchange is a fee collecting engine, and purchasing a share in said engine is by buying hype, which also serves as governance.

Also /biz/ is retarded these days, one-shotted by chainlink and not knowing about anything outside of the cult bubble.

61924972
>>61918597
>source, closed

61926502
>>61922017
it literally broke 20 in the january drop , it should be unhindered to high 40s or 50 into a quick retest of idk 35ish into ath or just straight to 60 over some weeks/months.

61926525
>>61924803
it's 2026. Ask AI about HYPE burn address and stop making shit up.

61927011
>>61926502
Nah this is moonboy posting. The regulatory and closed source fud are real and people are quick to point to the 11 man team making trillions of revenue a year as a bullish moat when any logical investor sees that as a massive target for competitors.

The team at least recognizes this hence the $28 million funding a lobbying team for US compliance. But on a strict regulatory angle Lighter edges Hype out simply due to the fact they're structured from the ground up as a US company on top of their VC connections

61927038
>>61927011
must be great for the lighter shareholders

61929634
>>61927011
The VC connections are precisely the problem with Lighter. Half the damn supply belongs to the team and VCs. Less than 3k people hold this shit and most of the circulating supply is in the top 10. Only 25% of supply is circulating. FDV is through the roof and it's done nothing but dump since it was launched. Once it's -85% it might be worth a look but not before

item.title: >Uuuhhh oil prices have being priced in for months — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>Uuuhhh oil prices have being priced in for months

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-03-03 16:00 UTC

OP

>so they arent going to rise
Why the fuck has every TA and their mum being saying this when Cleary 70 dollars oil is not pricing in 20% of the worlds oil coming to a halt?

Early replies (first 3)

61929065
>>61929061
what?

61929089
>>61929065
Wallstreet anyalsts and TA (trend anyalsts) have being saying for weeks prior to the February 28th strikes that even if a war breaks out in the middle east all of this geopolitical and supply chain risks are factored into its current price and it is already trading at a risk premium, which is insane considering 20% of the worlds easiest extractable oil has been effectively shut off and the only reason prices havent surged is due to a on market surplus as OPEC released its reserves

61929126
>>61929089
I hear you anon. This was the most retarded shit I’ve ever seen. Yesterday was a golden opportunity for anyone who wasn’t sucking Trump off or bone dead retarded to long oil or go short - because OBVIOUSLY shit is hyper fucked and it’s insane no one was acknowledging it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61929145
>>61929089
лyл

61929152
>>61929126
> hyper fucked and it’s insane no one was acknowledging it.
the second we use up the on market opec surplus is the second oil prices go bananas, as their wont be any in reserve and much less being extracted as half of it will be in a cloud drifting over asia

61929409
>>61929152
And how long will it take until the surplus runs out?

61929488
>>61929409
well thats the question 2 weeks (not doing the gay meme) is the estimate at the longest

61929505
Most of Asia's oil comes from the Middle East. They are thoroughly fucked. China is over

61929646
>>61929152
So what should I invest in for this?

61929753
>>61929646
anything the tracks oil prices or non middle eastern oil companies in Brazil or America, petrobras seems good so do XOM and CVX, OXY seems good as per $1 increase in the WTI oil price its cash flow increases by 240 million, if oil reaches 100 it will have a cash flow of 15 billion and should hopefully be able to pay off its crown rock acquisitions, and it has a break even price of 40 dollars whilst being concentrated in the Permian basin where as the average for domestic us producers is like 50 -60 but the risks with oxy are more so now as they sold off oxy chem for very little to Berkshire so they have kind of become a leveraged oil purchase, at least in my uneducated opinion

61929979
>>61929061
prices are already up, so what are we even talking about?

61924774
>>61924430
what ho, good morrow and fair tidings fellow gentleman of misaligned pigmentation. what folly is this bavarian pish posh on such a fine non-reflective day of work and trade?

>>61924467
das rite!

61927092
>>61924430
Hyperborian Jack will save the market

61929955
ARYAN TUESDAY

item.title: Oil price — 66 replies — 81.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Oil price

Open thread • Replies: 66 • Updated: 2026-03-03 15:14 UTC

OP

>Street of Hormuz de-facto closed for at 24h
>20-25% of global oil supply cut from market
>Refineries attacked and closed in Saudi Arabia
>Tankers attacked on/near Street of Hormuz
>Tanker insurance fees through the roof
>Regime wants no negotiations with US
>Army and Guards still loyal to Regime
>Bloomberg predicts prices around 100$/barrel Brent

Still Oil price is going down. Merely up 5% from Friday. What the fuck is going on?!

Early replies (first 3)

61925169
Priced in

61925191
>>61925117
>>Regime wants no negotiations with US
Nice try moshi

61925221
>>61925169
How can an indefinite cut of 20-25% of global oil supply be priced in with like 16$ increase since start of 2026?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61926020
>>61925906
It's not yet. That's the key. It's a bet on if this is going to keep going.

61926068
No more bullshit on CNBC. Iran says the strait is closed due to aids and any ship that passes through will be set on fire.

61926102
>>61926068
HOLY FUUUCCKIN SHIIT MY LONGS ARE POOOOOOOOOMPING

61926691
>>61925117
Price increases would cover insurance premiums going up. If the premiums go up but the price doesn't go up transporting oil becomes unprofitable. This could exacerbate any shortages in the long term.

61926787
>>61925117
>>61925169
>>61925191

Literally nothing is priced in. VIX is 21 and oil barely closed at 72. Market thinks this is a nothingburger.

If you disagree buy 10k worth of VIX or USO calls April expiry and take the 7x. That’s all there is to it. Shits not in the price.

61926796
>>61926691
https://infra.economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/ports-shipping/war-risk-premiums-surge-as-hormuz-crisis-forces-rerouted-sailings-threatening-indias-trade/128933982?dt=2026-03-02

61926861
>>61925117
Based. Total westoid humiliation.

61926893
Let's make this perfectly clear.

https://k.sina.cn/article_1887344341_707e96d504001qa2g.html
Translation:
>According to vessel tracking platform TankerTrackers.com, more than 50 tankers remain in Iranian waters, 18 of which are loaded with crude oil and 37 are empty.

>Premiums are soaring.

But as said in the link here: >>61926796

>On Sunday, GIC Re issued notice of cancellation for its marine hull war risk cover, stating that the cover will cease from March 3. The insurer has drawn up a high-risk area that includes Pakistan waters, Persian or Arabian Gulf and adjacent waters/ports including the Gulf of Oman west of Longitude 58 degree east, Iran and all other countries under sanction by the UN, the UK, the US or the EU, Sea of Azov and Black Sea waters, waters of Russia, Ukraine/ Belarus, India Ocean, Gulf of Aden, and Southern Red Sea.

>“In case any vessel passes through this area or calls any port located in this area or dry docked in any of these areas, it will be a breach of warranty,” GIC Re wrote in a circular issued on Sunday, adding that the breach of warranty cover will not be available in respect of all the seven zones mentioned in the high-risk area.

So if I get this right it didn't even matter that much that Iran actually attacked ships. The threat they made to do so going forward made it impossible to get insurance to pass through. So the strait is effectively closed not just by way of Iran's actions but because of the insurers. The market's optimism today was delusional. This news will spread.

61926901
>>61925534
It’s just a few airstrikes dude, it’s not that deep.

61926908
>>61926901
It won't take long for you to realize the economic damage done.

61926914
>>61926908
To them yeah, not over here. I’m just going to keep profiting from my oil tanker stocks in the meantime.

61926920
>>61926914
lol

lmao even

61926942
>>61925117
token not needed

61927578
>>61926893
Based.

61927593
>>61925522
Both Hegseth and Trump haven't ruled out boots on the ground

61927775
>>61926893
Yup, and like I said with the price increase covering the cost of insurance, if the price doesn't go up to compensate to the insurance even when the insurance is being issued, it won't be economically justifiable to take out a journey which purchases that insurance. A low price of oil will close the straight down until the price increases enough to justify running the blockade.

An unknown risk factor is also a refusal by crews to take those routes. It is unclear if there is any kind of danger pay, but I would imagine that workers might start demanding it tankers keep getting hit.

The price should be shooting up by a lot, but we psychologically in an end phase cycle of a bullmarket where people don't think bad things can happen. Rather than price responding to condition, conditions respond to price, so people will just act like "oh well I guess if the price didn't go up by that much it isn't a big deal" as the usefulness of a measuring stick (like GDP) is inversely correlated to how much focus actors place on it. If people are watching the oil price as a signal to determine how bad the war is going then the American government has reason to try to suppress the price for the same reason that it suppresses casualty figures. The price of oil right now is part of the propaganda. It is definitely going to wreak havoc eventually, but it isn't reacting because the reaction being known would negatively impact US foreign policy.

Instead of the price responding to the conditions, the conditions will respond to the price and keep the straight closed in practice even without Iran doing anything

61927855
>>61925117
>What the fuck is going on?!
World is in a economic recession.

61928364
So it seems the market is slowly waking up

61928463
It’s going up now and wont stop anytime soon
100-120$ is the target

61928504
Ha.
Where is the Ever Given when you need it?

61928516
>>61928504
Not needed. The straight is effectively closed now for at least 36h.
Brent just climbed to $83. Shits about to go down.

61928621
>>61928516
DHT is down
The fuck

61928772
>>61927775
Good post. I bought calls on the oil tanker futures ETF, BWET, as a hedge/speculation. Has exploded more than 5x and I'm looking at 10x.

61929116
WHY IS OIL DOWN!?

61929443
>>61925241
>not putting boots on the ground

61929484
>>61925169
is the recovery after peace settlement also priced in?

61929496
>>61925117
Burgers are currently crashing their own economy to prop the dollar index up and metals, oil and commodities down to make it look as if everything is still under their control and not a massive dumpster fire

61929543
>>61925438
Venezuela OPEC member is compensating

61929629
>>61929543
Yeah, Venezuela's dilapidated oil infrastructure/shit oil is going to save the day
